query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Extol', 'Praise enthusiastically', 'Scholars noted a striking extol between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Extort', 'To take by illegal exercise of power or ingenuity', 'Over time, the community learned to extort its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Extortion', 'Obtaining something  by the abuse of office or authority', 'The essay highlights the extortion of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Extradite', 'To move a person from one state to another by legal process', 'Over time, the community learned to extradite its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Extradition', 'Surrender of an alleged fugitive by one authority to another', 'Scholars noted a striking extradition between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Extrajudicial', 'Carried out without legal authority', 'Her extrajudicial became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Extraneous', 'Irrelevant or unrelated', 'The passage presents a extraneous portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Extraordinary', 'Not ordinary, exceptional, unusual', 'The reviewer described the tone as extraordinary, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Extrapolate', 'To infer or estimate based on known data', '“Scientists extrapolate climate trends from past data.”', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Extravagance', 'Excessive or superfluous expenditure of money', 'Scholars noted a striking extravagance between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Extravagant', 'Spending much more than is necessary or wise, wasteful', 'Her extravagant became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Extremist', 'Holding extreme views, especially on a political subject', 'Scholars noted a striking extremist between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Extremity', 'The most extreme or furthest point of something', 'Scholars noted a striking extremity between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Extricate', 'To free, disengage, loosen, or untangle', 'Over time, the community learned to extricate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Extrinsic', 'External, separable from the thing itself, inessential', 'Scholars noted a striking extrinsic between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Extrovert', 'An outgoing, gregarious person', 'The essay highlights the extrovert of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Extrude', 'To push or thrust out', 'Over time, the community learned to extrude its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Exuberance', 'Effusively and almost uninhibitedly enthusiastic', 'Her exuberance became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Exuberant', 'Filled with lively energy and excitement', 'The passage presents a exuberant portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Exude', 'To discharge through pores, to emit, to give out', 'Critics argued that the plan might exude the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Exult', 'To rejoice, be very happy', 'The author hoped the new policy would exult tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Fabricate', 'Invent falsely', 'Critics argued that the plan might fabricate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Fabrication', 'The act of framing, construction, manufacture', 'The essay highlights the fabrication of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Fabulous', 'Extraordinary, especially in being very large', 'The reviewer described the tone as fabulous, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Facade', 'The face of a building, especially the front', 'Her facade became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Facet', 'One of the small, polished plane surfaces of a cut gem', 'The essay highlights the facet of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Facetious', 'Not meant to be taken seriously or literally', 'Over time, the community learned to facetious its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Facile', 'Easily accomplished or attained', 'Scholars noted a striking facile between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Facilitate', 'To make an action or process easier', '“The new software facilitates communication between remote teams.”', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Facility', 'Ease in learning or in doing something, proficiency', 'Her facility became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Facsimile', 'To send via a facsimile machine, normally spoken as “fax”', 'Over time, the community learned to facsimile its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Faction', 'A group or clique within a larger group', 'Her faction became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Factionalism', 'The splitting of a group into factions', 'Her factionalism became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Factious', 'Of, pertaining to, or caused by factions', 'The reviewer described the tone as factious, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Fad', 'A temporary fashion, notion, manner of conduct, etc', 'Scholars noted a striking fad between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Falderol', 'Nonsense or foolishness', 'The essay highlights the falderol of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Fallacious', 'Based on a mistaken belief', 'The passage presents a fallacious portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Fallacy', 'Deceptive or false appearance, deceitfulness, deception', 'Scholars noted a striking fallacy between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Fallible', 'Capable of making mistakes or being wrong', 'Her fallible became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Fallow', 'To make land fallow for agricultural purposes', 'Critics argued that the plan might fallow the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Falter', 'To waver or be unsteady', 'Critics argued that the plan might falter the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Familiarize', 'To make oneself or someone else familiar with something', 'Over time, the community learned to familiarize its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Famine', 'Extreme and general scarcity of food, as in a country', 'The essay highlights the famine of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Famish', 'To suffer or cause to suffer extreme hunger, starve', 'Her famish became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Fanatic', 'A person with an extreme enthusiasm or zeal, as in religion', 'Her fanatic became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Fanatical', 'Having an extreme zeal or enthusiasm for something', 'What seemed fanatical at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Fanaticism', 'The characteristic or practice of being a fanatic', 'Scholars noted a striking fanaticism between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Fanciless', 'Having no fancy, without ideas or imagination', 'What seemed fanciless at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Farcical', 'Resembling a farce, ludicrous, absurd', 'Scholars noted a striking farcical between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Fastidious', 'Very attentive to detail and accuracy', '“She was fastidious about organizing her books by genre and author.”', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
